description: Phase-2 parity declarations for the matched Python client and TypeScript peer (clients/ts).

capabilities.reason: The TS client owns the bounded public error_category enum (clients/ts receipts, fixture ts-public-receipt-error-category-v1); the Python package exposes sanitized error text on its internal runtime receipt and does not own that taxonomy. The agent-receipt half of the shared receipt-shapes fixture exercises AgentMemory.record_receipt, which the TS client intentionally does not implement.

not_yet_extracted.scenario: fallback-runner command and receipt ordering matrix
not_yet_extracted.reason: The current assertions mix transport fallback mechanics with scope proof; phase 1 keeps those tests authoritative instead of inventing a runtime-neutral fallback command contract. The TS client has no mcp2cli fallback route at all.

not_yet_extracted.scenario: corrupt JSONL, fsync failure, short-write, symlink, and concurrent-append matrix
not_yet_extracted.reason: These are per-runtime spool implementation and filesystem durability details, not yet a matched-client wire contract.

not_yet_extracted.scenario: disclosure-bundle rendering and reserved-field rejection matrix
not_yet_extracted.reason: The golden disclosure fixture is a separate export contract; its existing tests remain authoritative until the TS client owns an equivalent export surface.
